paper_title: Climate and Vegetation Data for the Past

methods.method_name: Climate Data Downscaling and Bias Correction
methods.description: This method involves downscaling and bias-correcting coarse-resolution climate model outputs to create a high-resolution dataset that aligns with present-day observations. The delta method is used to adjust the model outputs based on observed data.

steps.description: The mean climate change signal, that is, past snapshot minus present-day snapshot, is added to the observations.
steps.searchable_summary: apply delta method to adjust climate model outputs

steps.description: The 'delta' is interpolated to the high resolution and then added to the high-resolution present data.
steps.searchable_summary: interpolate delta to high resolution and adjust present data

steps.description: Spatial downscaling and bias correction was applied to the HadCM3 climate variables.
steps.searchable_summary: apply spatial downscaling and bias correction to climate variables

methods.description: This method uses the BIOME4 model to simulate equilibrium vegetation types and net primary productivity (NPP) based on climate data inputs.

steps.description: BIOME4 simulates the competition between different plant functional types (PFTs) and assigns biomes based on the most successful and second most successful PFT and their leaf area index.
steps.searchable_summary: simulate vegetation types using BIOME4 model

methods.description: This method involves associating radiocarbon dates with paleoclimatic reconstructions and analyzing the ecological niches of horses using PCA and niche overlap metrics.

steps.description: Each radiocarbon date available was associated to its nearest paleoclimatic reconstruction, and the corresponding environmental variables and biome/vegetation coverage estimation for the location.
steps.searchable_summary: associate radiocarbon dates with paleoclimatic reconstructions

steps.description: Visualized the overlap between the observed climatic niches in Asia and Europe using a PCA performed in R.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA to visualize climatic niche overlap

steps.description: Quantified the niche overlap of the two subpopulations (European and Asian) in environmental space using Schoener's D.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate niche overlap using Schoener's D

steps.description: Randomly subsampled the horse occurrences of Europe in each time interval to match the number of available occurrences in the Asian database.
steps.searchable_summary: subsample European horse occurrences for comparison
